title: Lastrea stipulacea
intro: Lastrea stipulacea, also known as the Stipule Fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea stipulacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea stipulacea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Lastrea stipulacea is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to Asia and is also known by its synonym Athyrium stipulaceum. The genus Lastrea is a group of ferns that are commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Lastrea stipulacea is classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on the ground rather than on other plants. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies.
